Content: As a moderator, I can flag a voice contribution for review so toxic audio doesn't slip through because it wasn't typed

+ topics (→ on click, generate summary for each →)

I can see an AI-generated summary in a persistent side panel so I can follow the debate's key points without scrolling through everything

the summary panel updates automatically as new contributions arrive so I never need to refresh

As a moderator, I can see the reason a contribution was flagged (toxic, off_topic, duplicate) so I don't have to re-read it cold
As a moderator, I can dismiss a flag as a false positive so the AI learns what this community tolerates

Show summary, list of sources, and relevant unanswered questions

when accesing a debate, show a AI-generated slides:

text input where you can enter prompt (e.g. 'which of these posts talk about <topic>?') and will highlight those posts with some visual indicator. "Overlays" are stackable so you can visually map participation
when I submit a contribution I am warned if my text is very similar to an existing one so I don't repeat what's already been said

"overlays" / smart filters

Email and messaging summaries

Share clear, short summaries of what is happening on Decidim by email, WhatsApp, Signal, or SMS. This supports people who cannot log in regularly or who rely on mobile access. These messages should be transcripted on Decidim, to ensure compliance with ethical standards. Example: A weekly “What happened / What’s next” message with links for those who want to go deeper.
Audio and voice-based input

Allow participants to contribute via voice notes, phone calls, or recorded messages that organisers can later upload or summarise on Decidim (with consent). This can reduce barriers related to literacy, language, disability, or confidence.
Offer information in more than one way

Not everyone can or wants to engage only through a digital platform. Accessibility also means providing alternatives when needed: sharing summaries by email, allowing voice notes or offline input, offering extra time to participate, or translating key information. These low-tech solutions are especially important for people facing barriers related to disability, language, time, care responsibilities, or digital access.
Offer multiple, feasible ways to contribute (text, audio, video, visual) when possible
Consider what might be accessible formats like video or audio to share updates or outcomes
using Decidim as an archive
